title: Minuartia kashmirica
intro: Minuartia kashmirica, also known as Kashmirian minuartia, is a small, flowering plant belonging to the family Caryophyllaceae, native to the Himalayan region of Kashmir. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia kashmirica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its extracts are also used in the production of herbal supplements and pharmaceuticals.
taxonomy: Minuartia kashmirica is a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Minuartia and the order Caryophyllales. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other plants. The family Caryophyllaceae consists of approximately 85 genera, including Minuartia, Silene, and Dianthus. Minuartia kashmirica is a distinct species within this family.
